1121977712114941982.content:
Having been bullied a solid amount in my youth I can definitely identify with Stephie here. Sometimes when you are in a bad spot in life and looking for any way to feel alright, the last thing you need is someone ruffling your feathers or poking at your wounds. In these moments, that kind of treatment can really amplify the pain one is already going through and make a bad day much worse. Additionally, there is something therapeutic about receiving acceptance from trusted people or a trusted group and I believe that is what she was looking for when she posted that series of pictures.

That being said, I have learned over the years that there is quite a benefit to approaching oneself with a semi-detached perspective and avoiding taking things too personally. I think oversensitivity can make people as uncomfortable as insensitivity can and both, as expressions of self, can become ways of seeking power. Additionally, over-sensitivity really can damage intimacy, so I think a good portion of people might see something like Stephie’s sensitivity as making her hard to reach. So while something like Fib’s comment could be viewed as a petty bullying personal attack it could also be viewed as shit test and a covert mode of testing her personal resolve. If one had issue with the way Stephie is and wanted to make her aware of how she comes off it is hard to say what the ideal way to communicate it to her would be, but there is admittedly probably a better way to communicate it than a breaking bad reference.
